questions.question: What is the primary difference between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS?
options: They differ only in pricing models
They represent different levels of abstraction: IaaS provides infrastructure, PaaS adds platform management, SaaS delivers complete applications
IaaS is for storage, PaaS is for networking, SaaS is for compute
They are three names for the same cloud model
questions.explanation: IaaS gives you virtual machines and networking, PaaS adds runtime and middleware management, and SaaS provides fully managed applications, each abstracting more operational responsibility.

questions.question: What is an Availability Zone (AZ) in AWS?
options: A geographic area containing multiple regions
One or more discrete data centres with redundant power and networking within a region
A virtual private network segment
A pricing tier for reserved instances
questions.explanation: Each AZ is an isolated data centre facility within a region, designed so that failures in one AZ do not affect others, enabling high-availability architectures.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of serverless computing (e.g., AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions)?
options: It eliminates the need for any code
You pay only for actual compute time and do not manage servers, scaling is automatic
It guarantees zero latency for all requests
It provides dedicated bare-metal hardware
questions.explanation: Serverless platforms handle provisioning, scaling, and maintenance automatically. You are billed per execution, making it cost-effective for variable or event-driven workloads.

questions.question: What is Amazon S3 primarily used for?
options: Relational database management
Scalable object storage with high durability
In-memory caching
Container orchestration
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 (Simple Storage Service) provides virtually unlimited object storage with 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability, used for backups, static assets, data lakes, and more.

questions.question: What is the purpose of IAM (Identity and Access Management) in cloud platforms?
options: To monitor application performance metrics
To control who can access which cloud resources and what actions they can perform
To manage DNS routing
To encrypt data at rest
questions.explanation: IAM provides fine-grained access control by defining users, groups, roles, and policies that specify exactly which API actions are allowed on which resources.

questions.question: What is a VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)?
options: A type of virtual machine instance
A logically isolated virtual network within a cloud provider where you define your own IP ranges, subnets, and routing
A managed container service
A content delivery network
questions.explanation: A VPC gives you complete control over your virtual networking environment, including IP address ranges, subnets, route tables, and network gateways.

questions.question: What does auto-scaling do in a cloud environment?
options: Automatically updates your application code
Dynamically adjusts the number of compute instances based on demand metrics
Encrypts traffic between services
Backs up databases on a schedule
questions.explanation: Auto-scaling monitors metrics like CPU utilization or request count and adds or removes instances to match demand, optimizing both performance and cost.

questions.question: What is a CDN (Content Delivery Network) and why is it used?
options: A centralized database network for real-time analytics
A distributed network of edge servers that cache content close to end users to reduce latency
A type of load balancer for microservices
A CI/CD pipeline service
questions.explanation: CDNs like CloudFront, Cloud CDN, and Azure CDN cache static and dynamic content at edge locations worldwide, dramatically reducing load times for global users.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of managed database services (e.g., RDS, Cloud SQL)?
options: They are always free to use
The cloud provider handles backups, patching, scaling, and high availability, reducing operational burden
They only support NoSQL databases
They run exclusively on serverless infrastructure
questions.explanation: Managed databases offload operational tasks like patching, backups, failover, and replication to the provider, letting teams focus on application logic instead of database administration.

questions.question: What is the key difference between containers and virtual machines?
options: Containers include a full OS; VMs share the host kernel
Containers share the host OS kernel and are more lightweight; VMs include a full guest OS and hypervisor layer
There is no meaningful difference
VMs start faster than containers
questions.explanation: Containers virtualize at the OS level, sharing the host kernel, making them faster to start and more resource-efficient. VMs include a complete guest OS, providing stronger isolation.

questions.question: What is multi-cloud strategy?
options: Running everything on a single cloud provider in multiple regions
Using services from two or more cloud providers to avoid vendor lock-in, increase resilience, or leverage best-of-breed services
Using cloud and on-premises infrastructure together
Deploying the same application in multiple containers
questions.explanation: Multi-cloud distributes workloads across providers (e.g., AWS + GCP), reducing dependency on one vendor and allowing teams to choose the best service for each need.

questions.question: What is edge computing?
options: Running workloads exclusively in the cloud provider's largest data centres
Processing data closer to where it is generated, at the network edge, to reduce latency and bandwidth usage
A method for encrypting data at rest
A frontend JavaScript framework
questions.explanation: Edge computing moves computation to locations near data sources (IoT devices, users), enabling real-time processing and reducing the need to send all data to centralised cloud regions.

questions.question: What does a load balancer do?
options: It compresses data to reduce storage costs
It distributes incoming network traffic across multiple backend servers to ensure no single server is overwhelmed
It manages database replication
It schedules container deployments
questions.explanation: Load balancers (ALB, NLB, Cloud Load Balancing) spread traffic across healthy instances, improving availability, fault tolerance, and response times.

questions.question: What is a common strategy for cloud cost optimisation?
options: Always using the largest instance types available
Using reserved instances or savings plans for predictable workloads, and spot/preemptible instances for fault-tolerant jobs
Disabling auto-scaling to keep costs fixed
Running all services in a single availability zone
questions.explanation: Reserved instances offer up to 72% savings for steady-state workloads, while spot instances provide up to 90% savings for interruptible jobs. Right-sizing and auto-scaling further reduce waste.

questions.question: In cloud networking, what is a 'public subnet' vs a 'private subnet'?
options: Public subnets use IPv6; private subnets use IPv4
Public subnets have a route to an internet gateway allowing direct internet access; private subnets do not
Public subnets are free; private subnets cost extra
There is no technical difference; it is just a naming convention
questions.explanation: Resources in a public subnet can receive traffic directly from the internet via an Internet Gateway. Private subnet resources access the internet only through a NAT gateway, adding a layer of security.
